title: Trek ‘Pro’ Hoof Boot (sold per single boot)
text: Cavallo Trek PRO Hoof Boot SOLD AS SINGLE BOOTS (Available in Regular Sole, sizes 1-4) The Cavallo Trek PRO Hoof Boot is an upgrade to our best-selling classic Trek model. Designed for ultimate protection, comfort, and style, Trek PRO has a regular sole shape, ideal for horses with round hooves (equal width and length). Whether you’re navigating rocky trails, entering the show ring, using hoof boots for therapeutic reasons, or transporting your horse safely, Trek PRO delivers unmatched performance. Trusted worldwide, the Cavallo Trek PRO blends advanced materials with a sleek, moulded profile to stand out and hold up, ride after ride. Built to Perform. Designed to Impress. Trek PRO features a fully moulded Pro Mesh upper, exclusive to Cavallo, for a snug and stylish fit. The Pro Mesh is a high-performance thermoplastic urethane (TPU) material that’s both lightweight and incredibly strong. The unique honeycomb design enhances flexibility, allowing the upper to move and mould with the hoof, and offers a secure, custom fit for every horse. Moulded recesses protect the inset D-rings, reducing wear and ensuring long-term durability. This breathable, abrasion-resistant pro mesh allows heat and moisture to escape, helping to keep hooves cool, clean, and dry. Built to withstand rugged terrain, water crossings, and repeated use, Pro Mesh resists UV damage and does not trap bacteria. It

title: Your Cavallo News - June 2024
text: Your Cavallo News – June 2024 Ground-Breaking Research by Barefoot Horse Researcher, Sarah Albanozzo Sarah Albanozzo is a Cavallo Hoof Boot lover and a passionate believer in barefoot and booted lifestyle for optimal horse health. She is a Research Project Manager at the University of Malta and a part-time student at the Royal (Dick) School of Veterinary Studies at the University of Edinburgh. Sarah is also an independent researcher on equine hoof morphology and has been a devout equestrian for 42 years. Sarah has shown us that the hooves of barefoot-kept horses are capable of walking over hard ground due to their physiology. Since the results showed accelerated sole growth and since there is no information documented in equine veterinary literature, she compared the horse’s foot to the human foot, focusing on a particular mechanoreceptor that is responsible for promoting sole growth. The success of this study, however, was also due to frequent non-invasive trimming cycles (keeping hoof walls level with the sole and toes short) and the fact that the animals were on a 100% forage-based diet with 24/7 turnout, roaming over varied terrain. When comparing locomotion in barefoot and booted horses versus shod horses, the difference is very evident, as noted in the published study below. The peer-reviewed and published paper, Hoof Capability of Barefoot-Kept Horses and Ponies Walking

title: Cavallo Hoofcare Savings
text: Cavallo Hoofcare Savings Cavallo Hoofcare savings are significant for horse owners, as hoof care is one of the biggest ongoing expenses. Traditional metal shoeing requires regular farrier visits and specialized materials. By switching to a barefoot lifestyle with Cavallo Hoof Boots, you can drastically cut costs while still providing your horse with the comfort and protection they need. Cavallo Hoof Boots eliminate the ongoing costs of custom metal shoes, offering an affordable and effective solution for hoof care without compromising your horse's health or performance. title: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Navicular
h1: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Navicular Support and Rehabilitation
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Navicular Support and Rehabilitation Cavallo Hoof Boots for Navicular provide effective, long-term relief for horses suffering from Navicular Syndrome or disease. Designed to promote natural hoof movement and reduce pain, Cavallo Hoof Boots feature scientifically proven shock absorption technology. Perfect for daily turnout, trail riding, or rehabilitation, they help restore mobility and protect sensitive hooves. Explore how Cavallo Hoof Boots can support your horse's Navicular recovery journey today. Table of Contents Understanding Navicular in Horses Traditional Treatments vs. Rehabilitation Cavallo Hoof Boots for Navicular Relief C avallo Testimonials Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots Understanding Navicular in Horses Navicular is a painful hoof condition that affects the navicular bone and surrounding structures. This includes the ligaments, tendons, and the navicular bursa. Most commonly seen bilaterally in the front hooves, it can also occur in the hind feet or a single hoof. Symptoms often include a shortened stride, tripping, difficulty turning, reluctance to walk downhill, and toe-first landings. These symptoms may progress gradually, making early identification critical for effective management. People often use the term 'Navicular Disease' interchangeably with Navicular Syndrome. However, Navicular Syndrome is a broader term that includes various i

title: Cavallo Hoof Boots Durability and Lifespan
h1: Cavallo Hoof Boots Durability and Lifespan
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots Durability and Lifespan Cavallo Hoof Boots Durability and Lifespan depend on several factors, including terrain, frequency of use, and proper care. Under standard conditions, Cavallo Hoof Boots last well over 2 years, with many riders reporting that their boots last even longer. Regular maintenance and correct storage are key to extending their lifespan. Our boots are backed by the Best Hoof Boot Warranty in the business - 180 days from the date of purchase. Learn more about how to maximize the longevity of your Cavallo Hoof Boots and when to consider replacing them. Table of Contents Durability of Cavallo Hoof Boots Extend Your Cavallo Hoof Boots Lifespan When to Replace Your Cavallo Hoof Boots Cavallo Testimonial s Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots What is the Durability and Lifespan of Cavallo Hoof Boots? The lifespan of Cavallo Hoof Boots depends on various factors, including terrain, the speed and gaits of your horse, and how often the boots are used. These variables can all influence how well the boots hold up over time. For an average rider who uses the boots a few times a week, you can expect them to last around 2+ years. Hoof Trimming - Keeping your horse's hooves properly trimmed and in good shape can help the boots fit better and last longer. Proper fit is essential for extending the lifespan of the boots. title: Cavallo Hoof Boots Secure Fit
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots' Secure Fit Cavallo Hoof Boots' Secure Fit ensures that your boots stay on through mud, water, and rough terrain. Their patented conical shape mimics the natural hoof structure, while industrial-strength Velcro and a soft foam-lined upper provides a snug, secure hold. The boots fully enclose the hoof and protect the heel bulbs, preventing slipping or loss. A unique drainage system allows water and mud to flow through, stopping suction from pulling boots off. Customize the fit with Cavallo pads, wraps, or sleeves for added security. Designed for durability, Cavallo Hoof Boots keep your horse comfortable and protected on any ride. Their unique design mimics the natural shape of a horse’s hoof, providing a snug, secure hold. Moreover, the conical structure, combined with industrial-strength Velcro, prevents shifting or slipping. Whether riding through deep mud, rocky trails, or steep inclines, Cavallo Hoof Boots stay firmly in place. Each boot fully encloses the hoof, offering complete coverage and protection.
